Online English to Sindarin Elvish Translator

Convert and translate modern English prose into authentic historical or fictional Sindarin Elvish Translator scripts instantly. Adjust size and download transparent calligraphy stencils.

Input English Text
Output Translation Tengwar Mode

Interactive Calligraphy Exporter

Generate a custom PNG layout of the calligraphy script above. Ideal for tattoos, stickers, or engraving layouts.

Academic Guide & Vocabulary Mapping

Introduction to Sindarin Elvish

Sindarin is the primary Elvish tongue spoken in Middle-earth during the Third Age. Unlike Quenya, which became a language of lore and high ceremony, Sindarin was the daily language of the Grey-elves (Sindar). Tolkien based the phonology and grammar of Sindarin on Welsh, giving it a soft, Celtic quality with complex vowel mutations.

Our translator utilizes a verified academic dictionary matching real vocabulary words like meleth (love), mellon (friend), and estel (hope). For words not in the lexicon, it falls back to a precise Tengwar character transliteration mapping, allowing you to design rings and tattoos seamlessly.

Core Verified Dictionaries

Here are some of the most requested concepts mapped to their verified translation values in our database:

English Concept Sindarin Elvish Translator Script / Notation
love meleth
friend mellon
star el
water nen
sun anor
moon ithil
shadow dae
dark mór
light calen
flower loth
shield eramath
king aran
hope estel
heart gûr
gold glor

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

What is the difference between Quenya and Sindarin?

Quenya is high Elvish, comparable to Latin in our world, used for formal writing and ceremony. Sindarin is the common spoken tongue of the Elves of Middle-earth during the Third Age (LOTR era).

Can I use Sindarin for a custom wedding ring engraving?

Yes! Sindarin is the most popular language for Lord of the Rings themed ring engravings. Phrases like 'meleth' (love) or 'estel' (hope) are highly authentic and elegant.

← Back to Elvish Calligraphy Board

Request Translations & Join Discussions

Need assistance transliterating custom quotes? Share your requests below and our community linguists will verify it.
